senses.definition: A person or thing that is the fulfillment or later form of an earlier model or symbol, especially in religious or literary discussion.
examples: Many Christians see the New Testament figure as the antitype of the Old Testament hero.
In the lecture, the professor explained how the character works as an antitype to the older myth.

senses.definition: Plural of antitype: people or things that are fulfillments or later forms of earlier models or symbols.
examples: The writer described several characters as antitypes of ancient heroes.
In theology, these events are understood as antitypes of Old Testament stories.

senses.definition: Designed to prevent or fight typhoid fever.
examples: The doctor recommended an antityphoid vaccine before the trip.
Antityphoid measures greatly reduced the number of cases in the region.

senses.definition: Relating to or being an antitype; corresponding to an earlier model or symbol.
examples: The novel presents an antitypic figure who completes the earlier legend.
These antitypic images are common in religious art.

senses.definition: Serving as or relating to an antitype; matching or completing an earlier symbol or model.
examples: The ceremony has an antitypical meaning in modern practice.
Scholars debate the antitypical role of this character in the story.

senses.definition: In a way that relates to an antitype or fulfills an earlier model or symbol.
examples: The event is antitypically linked to the older festival.
The figure can be understood antitypically rather than literally.

senses.definition: Having the nature of an antitype; corresponding to or completing an earlier pattern or symbol.
examples: The antitypous figure appears in later versions of the myth.
Some rituals have antitypous meanings that reflect older traditions.

senses.definition: The relationship between a type and its antitype, especially in religious or symbolic interpretation.
examples: The sermon focused on the antitypy between Old and New Testament events.
Scholars discussed the antitypy in the structure of the epic poem.

senses.definition: Acting against or blocking the action of the enzyme tyrosinase.
examples: The cream contains antityrosinase agents to reduce dark spots.
Researchers are testing new antityrosinase compounds for skin care.

senses.definition: Not recognized as a standard word in contemporary English; likely a misspelling or extremely rare technical form.
examples: The form "antiuating" does not appear in major modern dictionaries.
Learners should avoid using "antiuating" in formal writing.

senses.definition: Used to prevent or treat ulcers, especially in the stomach.
examples: The doctor prescribed an antiulcer medication for her stomach pain.
Antiulcer drugs have reduced the number of serious stomach ulcers.

senses.definition: Aimed at reducing or preventing unemployment.
examples: The government introduced antiunemployment policies during the crisis.
Antiunemployment programs focused on training young workers.

senses.definition: Opposed to or hostile toward labor unions.
examples: The company has a strong antiunion culture.
Workers complained about antiunion policies at the factory.

senses.definition: A person who is opposed to labor unions.
examples: Antiunionists argued that unions would hurt the company.
He is known as a leading antiunionist in the industry.

senses.definition: Plural of antiuniversity: organizations or movements that are opposed to traditional universities or university education, or alternative institutions set up in reaction to them.
examples: In the 1960s, several antiuniversities were founded as radical learning spaces.
Antiuniversities challenged the authority of traditional academic institutions.

senses.definition: Opposed to or hostile toward universities or university education.
examples: The antiuniversity movement criticized elite academic culture.
Some protestors carried antiuniversity banners.

senses.definition: A radical or alternative educational project created in opposition to traditional universities.
examples: She helped to set up an antiuniversity in London in the late 1960s.
The antiuniversity offered open seminars and workshops for the public.

senses.definition: Preventing or treating conditions caused by uric acid, such as gout.
examples: The patient was given an antiuratic drug for his gout.
Antiuratic therapy helped reduce her joint pain.

senses.definition: Showing opposition or negative feelings toward cities or city life.
examples: The politician's antiurban remarks upset many city residents.
Antiurban attitudes increased as people moved to the countryside.

senses.definition: Blocking or reducing the activity of the enzyme urease.
examples: The researchers tested antiurease compounds in the laboratory.
Antiurease activity was measured in the plant extracts.

senses.definition: Opposed to charging very high or unfair interest on loans.
examples: Anti-usurious laws were introduced to protect poor borrowers.
The group campaigns for antiusurious regulations in the banking sector.

senses.definition: Opposed to utilitarian philosophy, especially the idea that actions are right if they bring the greatest happiness to the greatest number.
examples: Her antiutilitarian views put more weight on individual rights than on overall happiness.
The book gives an antiutilitarian critique of modern economics.

senses.definition: The belief or position that opposes utilitarian ethics.
examples: Antiutilitarianism stresses duties and rights over total happiness.
The article traces the history of antiutilitarianism in moral philosophy.

senses.definition: Opposed to vaccination or to specific vaccines.
examples: Antivaccination groups spread false information online.
The government launched a campaign to counter antivaccination messages.

senses.definition: Opposition to vaccination in general.
examples: Antivaccination has become a serious public health concern.
The rise of antivaccination has led to new outbreaks of disease.

senses.definition: A person who is strongly opposed to vaccination.
examples: Antivaccinationists organized protests outside the clinic.
She debated an antivaccinationist on live television.

senses.definition: A person who rejects or fights against vaccination.
examples: Antivaccinators used social media to spread their claims.
The doctor tried to talk with the antivaccinator about the risks.

senses.definition: A person who is opposed to vaccination.
examples: Antivaccinists have existed since the earliest days of vaccines.
The article explains how antivaccinists argue against immunization.

senses.definition: Used to prevent or treat smallpox.
examples: Antivariolous measures were crucial before smallpox was eradicated.
Doctors once relied on antivariolous vaccines to save lives.

senses.definition: Preventing or counteracting poisoning.
examples: Antivenefic remedies were described in early medical texts.
The herb was believed to have antivenefic properties.

senses.definition: A substance used to neutralize a poison or venom, especially from a snake or other animal.
examples: The hospital keeps antivenene for snake bites.
Researchers developed a new antivenene for scorpion stings.

senses.definition: Plural of antivenene: substances used to neutralize venoms or poisons.
examples: Different antivenenes are required for different species of snakes.
The lab tests antivenenes for their safety and strength.

senses.definition: Preventing or treating sexually transmitted diseases.
examples: Antivenereal campaigns helped reduce infection rates.
The clinic offered antivenereal advice and treatment.

senses.definition: A drug made from antibodies that is used to treat bites or stings from poisonous animals.
examples: The doctor quickly injected antivenin after the snake bite.
Without antivenin, the patient might not have survived.

senses.definition: Variant spelling of antivenin: a medicine used to treat venomous bites and stings.
examples: Older medical books sometimes use the form "antivenine".
In remote clinics, antivenine is kept for snake-bite emergencies.

senses.definition: Plural of antivenin: medicines used to treat venomous bites and stings.
examples: The hospital stores different antivenins for local snake species.
Researchers compared several antivenins in clinical trials.

senses.definition: A drug that contains antibodies and is used to treat bites or stings from poisonous animals.
examples: The ranger carried antivenom in case of snake bites.
Antivenom must be given quickly after a serious sting.

senses.definition: Counteracting or neutralizing venom.
examples: Researchers studied the antivenomous properties of the plant.
The serum has strong antivenomous effects.

senses.definition: Plural of antivenom: drugs used to treat venomous bites and stings.
examples: Different antivenoms are needed for different types of snakes.
The clinic stocked several antivenoms for emergencies.

senses.definition: Destroying or expelling worms, especially in the body.
examples: Antivermicular medicines are used to treat intestinal parasites.
The farmer used an antivermicular treatment for the animals.

senses.definition: Designed to reduce or stop vibration.
examples: The machine rests on antivibrating pads.
Antivibrating mounts help protect the equipment.

senses.definition: A device that reduces or removes vibration.
examples: The engine is fitted with an antivibrator to reduce noise.
They installed an antivibrator under the platform.

senses.definition: Preventing or reducing vibration.
examples: The antivibratory supports protect the structure.
Antivibratory materials were used under the floor.

senses.definition: Working against immoral or illegal behavior such as gambling, drug use, or prostitution.
examples: The city created an antivice task force.
Antivice campaigns targeted illegal gambling dens.

senses.definition: Opposed to the use of physical force or aggression.
examples: Antiviolence groups marched through the city.
The school launched an antiviolence program for teenagers.

senses.definition: Used to treat or prevent infections caused by viruses.
examples: The patient was given an antiviral drug for the flu.
Antiviral treatment should start as early as possible.

senses.definition: Protecting a computer against viruses.
examples: Make sure your laptop has up-to-date antiviral software.
Antiviral programs can scan email attachments for threats.

senses.definition: A drug used to treat or prevent a viral infection.
examples: Doctors prescribed an antiviral to shorten the illness.
This antiviral is effective against several strains of the virus.

senses.definition: Drugs used to treat or prevent viral infections.
examples: Antivirals can reduce the length of some illnesses.
Hospitals increased their stock of antivirals during the outbreak.

senses.definition: Acting against or destroying viruses.
examples: The lab is testing a new antivirotic compound.
Antivirotic agents are important in treating serious infections.

senses.definition: A drug or substance that fights viruses.
examples: The doctor chose an antivirotic with few side effects.
New antivirotics are being developed for emerging diseases.

senses.definition: Protecting a computer against viruses.
examples: You should install antivirus software on your computer.
The company bought new antivirus tools for its network.

senses.definition: A program that protects a computer from viruses.
examples: My antivirus stopped a dangerous file.
Free antivirus can still give basic protection.

senses.definition: Different antivirus programs used to protect computers against viruses.
examples: There are many antiviruses available for home users.
Reviews compare antiviruses on speed and protection.

senses.definition: A person who rejects the idea that living things have a special life force separate from physical and chemical processes.
examples: Antivitalists argue that biology can be explained by physics and chemistry.
The scientist was an outspoken antivitalist in his time.

senses.definition: Relating to the rejection of special life forces in living things.
examples: His antivitalist views shaped modern biology.
The paper offers an antivitalist explanation of life.

senses.definition: Rejecting or criticizing the idea that life depends on a special non-physical force.
examples: The text presents an antivitalistic account of organic processes.
Antivitalistic thinking became dominant in twentieth-century biology.
